rulings.Case Title: DONALD LEONARD BAAS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TAMMIE COIT et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES, LTD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, or in the alternative summary adjudication. Motion for Summary Judgment and Summary Adjudication denied as to issues number 1 and 2. Triable issue of fact regarding exposure. (Hansen deposition.) Summary Adjudication granted as to issue number 3. Strict products liability has replaced the implied warranty claim where there is no privity. (See Alvarez vs. Felker Mfg. Co. (1964) 230 Cal.App. 2nd 987, 1005.) No triable issue of fact raised establishing an express warranty. Summary Adjudication granted as to issue 4, no triable issue of fact raised that defendant made a false representation to decedent. Summary Adjudication denied as to issues number 5 and 6. Exhibits to Brown declaration raise triable issue of fact that defendant had knowledge of the dangers of asbestos but continued to sell the product. Defendant's objections overruled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DWIGHT FOSTER VS. SERVICESOURCE INTERNATIONAL, LLC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Servicesource International, Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 13, Defendant Servicesource International, Inc.'s Motion for summary judgment, per 170.6 Challenge to the Honorable Peter J. Busch. Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Regarding age discrimination, Plaintiff fails to establish a prima facie case. Lack of foundation that King took over his "meaningful work." Plaintiff was not replaced, and several remaining employees took over his work. Defendant provided a non-discriminatory reason for firing plaintiff. Three supervisors stated plaintiff caused problems due to his insubordination (Burns paragraph 3, Martinelli paragraph 3, and 10, Rosenberg paragraph 28. No evidence of pretext. Regarding retaliation, prima facie case not established because no temporal proximity between complaint of harassment and termination. Eight months not a relatively short time. Defendant established its non-discriminatory basis for the termination. No substantial evidence that the employer's reasons are untrue. Plaintiff's threat of litigation not considered as the allegation was not contained in the complaint and was not alleged in interrogatory answers. Regarding harassmsent, Plaintiff's 3 allegations of harassment not sufficiently severe or pervasive. Evidence, including emails, and totality of the circumstances indicate comments are sophomoric banter common in plaintiff's workplace. Hostile work environment claim fails, the negligent hiring claims also fail because there was no harassment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAY ELLIOT VS. DATA TRANSFER OF NEVADA, LLC, FORMERLY MIGO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, ROBERT HALLIGAN, and, DATA TRANSFER, LLC DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to first cause of action for breach of contract and covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action for fraud and third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff must clearly plead that the actions that form the basis of these claims occurred after the execution of the separation agreement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. RICHARD BOGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer To Richard Bogan'S Cross-Complaint, And Request For Attorney'S Fees Under Civ.C. 1780(E); Request For Judicial Notice Supporting Spark Construction, Inc.'S Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 8, PLAINTIFF SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. Demurrer To Richard Bogan's Cross-Complaint, and Request For Attorney's Fees. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Cross-complainant must plead the fraud with specificity. Request for attorney fees denied. Cross-defendant fails to establish the prosecution of the CLRA action was not in good faith. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT DONALD PLANSKY MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike is off calendar as moot in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ZHEN ZHUO PENG et al VS. DONALD PLANSKY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT DONALD PLANSKY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Notice is ambiguous at Page five. Owners state they intend to reside in their current house (671 22nd Avenue) until the "Subject Property" is renovated and available for their occupancy. Notice describes the "Subject Property" as 596 20th Avenue. Owners also state they intend to occupy 590 20th Avenue as their principal residence. Relative seeks in good faith to take possession of 596 20th Avenue along with the owners, if possible, and to move in simultaneously with the owners. San Francisco Rent Ordinance 37.9(8)(vi) prohibits plaintiff's recovery and occupancy of the "Subject Property", because they have previously recovered possession of 590 20th Avenue. =(302/CWW)
